Notice Of Motion And Motion For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement, Attorneys Fees, Costs, And Enhancement Payments; ***Court Reporter Fees Paid***
Off Calendar Jul-13-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Joanna Ghosh, Esq. (818-265-1020) of Lawyers for Justice, PC for Plaintiffs.
Boris Sorsher, Esq. (949-798-2101; bsorsher@fisherphillips.com) of Fisher & Phillips LLP for Defendants Dolls Kill Inc. and TriNet Group, Inc.
Roy K. Suh, Esq. (310-395-2988; roy@kicklawfirm.com) of The Kick Law Firm, APC for Class Member, Curbelo.

Hearing is held (as fully reported in the reporter's notes). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFFS NEQUASHA POTTS POTTS and MARQUIS BERRY's Motion For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement, Attorneys Fees, Costs, And Enhancement Payments:

The motion for final approval is granted on the condition that the class representative enhancement payments be reduced from $7,500 to $5,000 for each of the three named plaintiffs, with the difference ($7,500) to be included in the Net Settlement Amount to be distributed to class members. The Court finds this amount to be reasonable in light of the amount of time (approximately 30 hours each) and effort spent in connection with the litigation by each of the named plaintiffs. (See, e.g., Fronda v. Staffmark Holdings, Inc. (N.D. Cal. June 1, 2018), 2018 WL 2463101, at *13-*15 [reducing requested service award for class representative from $10,000 to $5,000].)

The request by class member Punipuao Curbelo for "clarification" of the scope of the PAGA release in the settlement agreement is denied. Curbelo has neither opted out of the settlement nor objected to it. This Court does not believe that there is any ambiguity in the scope of the release that requires clarification. Nor will the Court issue an advisory opinion regarding the possible effect of the settlement agreement on Curbelo's claims in other pending litigation.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041, email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. =(302/EPS)

The Court and counsel discuss scheduling the date for the final approval hearing.

Plaintiffs' motion for preliminary approval of the class action settlement, as amended, and of the revised proposed class notice, is granted.

Plaintiffs' are to email a proposed order to the Court for review and signature.
